deliverable1_envelope_map.chain_law: E[T](c) = 1 + sum_{d=1..7} c^d  (depth-7 top-4 survival product; stark #337)
deliverable1_envelope_map.envelope_law: envelope_X(c) = X_ANCHOR * E[T](c) / E[T](0.9213); X in {central=520.95, worst=492.87}, both anchored at E[T]=6.1112

deliverable2_inverse_solve.note: monotone bisection on env_X(c)=500; E[T](c) strictly increasing -> unique root. c*_central solves the (cap-bound) central envelope; since 500 < cap 520.95 the root lies BELOW the bar where central scales (above the bar central is flat at the ceiling, irrelevant to a 500 crossing).

deliverable3_ordering.verdict: identity bar is SUFFICIENT for central-500 (c*_central=0.9089 <= 0.9213) but NOT sufficient for worst-case 500 (c*_worst=0.9256 > 0.9213): clearing the strict greedy-identity bar buys central-500, while private-stable worst-500 demands a STRICTER coverage.

deliverable4_headroom_shortfall.note: central-500 sits 0.0124 of coverage BELOW the identity bar (the bar gives slack); worst-500 sits 0.0043 ABOVE it. lawine #336's +0.031 retrain lands the head exactly at 0.9213, so worst-500 needs 0.0043 MORE coverage than the retrain delivers (total lift 0.0353 from the honest 0.8903 prior, vs the +0.031 budget). The shortfall is small (13.9% of the retrain budget) but NONZERO: a worst-500 retrain must TARGET c*_worst, not merely the identity bar.

deliverable5_subcliff_structure.lever_argument: the inverse map scales the envelope at the SUB-CLIFF W=4 point (step x1). stark #337 proved widening to restore E[T] crosses the M=32->33 cliff (mu=1.16981 > mu_tie=1.10759), a net loss -- so the ONLY lever for higher coverage is a BETTER HEAD. Hence c*_central / c*_worst are HEAD-COVERAGE targets a retrain must hit at fixed W=4, not tree-width changes.
deliverable5_subcliff_structure.operating_point: sub-cliff W=4 (M=29 <= knee 32), verify step x1

rank_coverage_map.color_note: c*_worst needs top-4 rank coverage 0.6722 > the deployed LINEAR spine's 0.6532: worst-500 asks for a head STRONGER than the linear spine itself.
rank_coverage_map.note: c is per-depth effective acceptance c_eff; underlying top-4 rank coverage via salvage cov=(c_eff-a1)/(1-a1), a1=0.7731 (color only, NOT the ordering axis)

deliverable6_decision.binding_constraint_for_central_500: identity_bar 0.9213 (c*_central=0.9089 <= bar) -- clearing greedy-identity IS the central-500 target
deliverable6_decision.binding_constraint_for_worst_500: c*_worst=0.9256 > identity_bar -- worst-500 needs a STRICTER coverage than greedy identity

greedy_safety.argmax_note: coverage/E[T] is the SPEED axis: the draft acceptance rate changes HOW FAST tokens are verified, not WHICH token is emitted -- the target model's argmax remains the emitted token, so greedy identity is invariant to c. No served file, kernel, or decode path is touched.

deliverable6_decision.retrain_sizing: if the human's #319 retrain targets CENTRAL-500, set coverage target = identity bar 0.9213 (the +0.031 lawine #336 lift suffices); if it targets WORST-case (private-stable) 500, set coverage target = c*_worst=0.9256, i.e. +0.0043 beyond the identity bar and +0.0353 total from the honest 0.8903 prior (just past the +0.031 budget).
deliverable6_decision.supply_caveat_fern335: coverage is the DEMAND axis ONLY. fern #335 (5pos499e) found the binding axis is SUPPLY: EVEN at c*_worst the supply floor must still revive (phi>=0.255) -- coverage alone never reaches 500. c* is a NECESSARY demand-side target, not sufficient on its own.

synthesis.handoff: the SPEED-500 envelope needs coverage c*_central=0.9089 (central) / c*_worst=0.9256 (worst), so the ordering is c*_central < 0.9213 < c*_worst -> clearing the strict identity bar buys central-500 but is NOT sufficient for worst-case 500 (shortfall 0.0043 vs lawine #336's +0.031 retrain budget, total lift 0.0353 just past it), and EVEN at c*_worst supply must still revive (fern #335, phi>=0.255) -- coverage alone never reaches 500.

caveats: INVERSE of stark #337: this re-prices NOTHING measured -- it inverts the SAME banked envelope map (anchors x E[T] lever ratio) to find the coverage c* at env=500. No EAGLE-3 fusion checkpoint runs here; NOT a running EagleProposer.

the central anchor is CAP-BOUND at the lambda ceiling 520.95. Above the identity bar the central envelope is FLAT at the ceiling (a higher E[T] cannot exceed the cap), so envelope_central(c) scaling is the load-bearing model ONLY below the bar -- exactly where the 500 crossing (c*_central) lies. The worst corner is uncapped and scales throughout.
c is per-depth effective acceptance c_eff (the E[T](c) axis), in the SAME units as the identity bar 0.9213 and cov prior 0.8903 and lawine #336's +0.031. The rank-cov map (D5) is color only.

DEMAND axis only: c* is a NECESSARY coverage target; fern #335's SUPPLY floor (phi>=0.255) is the complementary binding constraint -- coverage alone never reaches 500. NOT a launch / build / served-file change / HF Job / submission / open2.
imports.provenance: stark #337 lbuirkpt (E[T](c)=1+sum_{d=1..7} c^d chain law, anchors central 520.95 / worst 492.87 @E[T]=6.1112, honest corners 470.35/444.99 @0.8903, cliff mu=1.16981, mu_tie=1.1076) x lawine #330 hfrscdai (cov prior 0.8903, identity bar 0.9213) x lawine #336 krroookz (+0.031 retrain lift) x fern #335 5pos499e (supply floor phi>=0.255). All run-ids in wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai.
